title: Fixing Invalid Height Calculation in a Binary Tree

statement.markdown: Given a binary tree where each node contains an integer value, calculate the height of the tree. The height of a binary tree is the number of edges on the longest path from the root to a leaf.
statement.inputFormat: A binary tree represented as an array of integers, where each integer represents a node's value.
statement.outputFormat: The height of the binary tree.
statement.notes: The height of a tree with a single node is 0.

examples.explanation: The height of the binary tree is the number of edges on the longest path from the root to a leaf.
